content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers. You are allowed to **rearrange** the elements of the array in any order.

A segment [l,r][l, r][l,r] of the array is considered beautiful if, for **every** pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) where l≤i≤j≤rl \leq i \leq j \leq rl≤i≤j≤r, either Aimod  Aj=0A_i \mod A_j = 0Ai​modAj​=0 or Ajmod  Ai=0A_j \mod A_i = 0Aj​modAi​=0. Note that `mod` denotes the modulo operation.

The score of a beautiful segment [l,r][l, r][l,r] is calculated as follows:


`score`([l,r])=min⁡(Al,Al+1,…,Ar)⋅(r−l+1)([l, r]) = \min(A_l, A_{l + 1}, \ldots, A_r) \cdot (r - l + 1)([l,r])=min(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​)⋅(r−l+1)
Your task is to determine the **maximum possible score** of a beautiful segment within the given array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the maximum possible score of a beautiful segment.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
The sum of max⁡(Ai)\max(A_i)max(Ai​) over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The array elements can be rearranged as [3,6,2,4][3, 6, 2, 4][3,6,2,4].
The beautiful segments are [1,1],[2,2],[3,3],[4,4],[1,2],[2,3],[3,4][1,1], [2,2], [3,3], [4,4], [1,2], [2,3], [3, 4][1,1],[2,2],[3,3],[4,4],[1,2],[2,3],[3,4].
score([1,2])=min⁡(A1,A2)⋅2=6([1, 2]) = \min(A_1, A_2) \cdot 2 = 6([1,2])=min(A1​,A2​)⋅2=6, which is the maximum possible score.
Test case 222: The array elements can be rearranged as [4,4,8,4,10][4, 4, 8, 4, 10][4,4,8,4,10].
The beautiful segment [1,4][1, 4][1,4] has the maximum score of 161616.
